title: QA Dead Reckoning — T-operator exact navigation on mod-24 lattice
_fixture_purpose: Certifies the QA T-operator as an exact dead reckoning engine. Four route witnesses demonstrate: (1) T^k iteration matches T^k matrix exponentiation exactly, (2) Pisano periodicity (T^24 = identity for (1,1) mod 24), (3) classical float DR accumulates error while QA has zero drift, (4) chromogeometric triple C²+F²=G² holds for every direction. Compass rose shows orbit classification of principal winds.
_derivation: Classical DR: x_{k+1} = x_k + d·sin(θ), y_{k+1} = y_k + d·cos(θ). Each leg applies sin/cos with float error ε ~ 10^{-10} to 10^{-15}. After N legs, accumulated error ~ ε·√N·d. QA DR: (b_{k+1},e_{k+1}) = T·(b_k,e_k) mod m where T=[[0,1],[1,1]]. After k legs: T^k via integer matrix exponentiation. Error = 0 identically — no float arithmetic in QA state evolution. The ONLY error source is the observer projection at input (continuous bearing → QA direction) and output (QA state → chart position). Theorem NT: boundary crossed exactly twice.

drift_comparison._note: Classical: 100 legs × 1000m at 45° with 1e-10 trig noise → 1.69 μm drift. QA: 0 by construction.

chromogeometry._note: Direction (3,4): G=d²+e²=25, F=d²-e²=-7, C=2de=24. C²+F²=576+49=625=25²=G². The green quadrance C=24=mod. Not coincidence.

witnesses.label: Long route: T^1000 on (3,5) mod 24
witnesses._note: T^1000 = T^(1000 mod 24) = T^16. Matrix exponentiation and iteration agree exactly. 1000 steps, zero drift.

drift_comparison._note: Classical: 10000 legs extrapolation → 5.56 μm drift. Grows as √N. QA: fixed at 0.
500 legs, trig table noise → ~3.94 μm classical drift.

witnesses.label: Full Pisano cycle: T^48 on (1,1) mod 9
witnesses._note: Two complete Pisano periods: π(9)=24, so T^48=T^0=identity. The navigator returns to exactly the starting point — impossible with float DR over 48 legs.

drift_comparison._note: Classical: 48-leg closed route should return to origin but doesn't due to float error.

chromogeometry._note: Direction (1,1): G=2, F=0, C=2. C²+F²=4+0=4=2²=G². F=0 means this direction is a null direction in the red (Minkowski) metric — the 45° diagonal is light-like.

compass_rose_mod24.label: Fixed (singularity)
compass_rose_mod24._note: Period 1. No bearing — the navigator's fixed point (9,9) mod 9 = (24,24) mod 24.

qa_connection.t_operator_is_dr: T^k·(b₀,e₀) mod m = exact position after k discrete navigation legs. No trig, no float, no drift.
qa_connection.pisano_is_circumnavigation: Pisano period π(m) = number of legs to return to start. π(24)=24, π(9)=24. A closed circuit of π(m) legs returns to exact origin.
qa_connection.chromo_is_triple_fix: Every direction (d,e) simultaneously provides: blue/Euclidean (GPS-like), red/Minkowski (LORAN-like), green/area (cross-track). C²+F²=G² guarantees consistency.
qa_connection.compass_is_orbit: The 3 QA orbit types partition compass bearings: cosmos (full navigation), satellite (reduced winds), singularity (no bearing).
qa_connection.theorem_nt: Continuous lat/lon is observer projection ONLY. QA state evolution (b,e)→(e,b+e) mod m is purely discrete. Boundary crossed exactly twice: at embarkation (continuous→QA) and at landfall (QA→continuous).

sources: QA T-operator: B. Iverson, Pythagorean Families (source texts)
Rational Trigonometry: N.J. Wildberger, Divine Proportions (2005)
Berggren tree: Berggren (1934), Barning (1963) — discrete geodesic navigation
